methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Built Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Noah Ready-Campbell and Andrew Liang, has raised $112M to date. Valuation analysis draws on two methods triangulated against a peer universe of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising eight private firms and one public comparable (277810.KQ). Key private peers include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to a $4M base value proxy derived from total capital raised, yielding $7M. Peer-median triangulation across nine comparable firms yields $1.6B, reflecting the median valuation of the peer set with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The peer-median approach carries greater weight given the availability of direct stage and sector comparables, particularly Hadrian at identical Series C stage and $1.6B valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $6M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median point of $803M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around capital efficiency relative to peers and the early-stage nature of robotics ventures at Series C. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to comparables and path to profitability in a capital-intensive sector.
